title: Executive Order 8233—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Enforcement of the Neutrality of the United States

flags.justification: Enforcement responsibility is distributed by jurisdiction across War, Navy, Treasury/Commerce, the Governor of the Panama Canal, Justice, and 'all departments and independent offices' — the opposite of concentration.

Each department's enforcement lane is defined by subject matter and territory (land forces to War; naval/hostile vessels to Navy; other vessels to Treasury/Commerce; Canal Zone to its Governor; residual enforcement and prosecution to Justice), so responsibility for a given failure is traceable to a specific department.
Single coherent purpose: enforcement of the neutrality proclamation of September 5, 1939.

Enforcement lanes are assigned by concrete, checkable criteria (type of vessel, branch of armed force, territory) rather than open-ended standards. Justice's residual clause covers only what is 'not especially delegated,' a narrow catch-all rather than a vague standard.
No incentive structure identifiable that rewards behavior contrary to enforcing neutrality. Sub-flag does not apply — the order does not invoke an open-ended emergency.

No explicit sunset clause, but the order is self-limiting by its own terms: duties apply 'during said war' (the declared neutrality period), which functions as an automatic termination condition tied to an external, verifiable event.
No existing oversight mechanism is reduced or removed; the order allocates enforcement duties already contemplated by the underlying neutrality statute and proclamation.

Interagency wartime coordination directed at foreign vessels and forces, not a domestic regulatory compliance scheme with a monitorable regulated party. The flag's compliance-monitoring frame does not map onto this instrument.
A narrow, technical distribution of pre-existing statutory enforcement duties among departments; nothing in the text enables, legitimizes, or forecloses activity beyond the stated purpose.

The order affirmatively carves out exceptions to avoid jurisdictional overlap (e.g., Navy's paragraph 2 duties are 'except as provided in paragraph numbered 4 hereof'; Treasury/Commerce's paragraph 3 duties exclude vessels already assigned in paragraph 2). This is evidence of deliberate conflict-avoidance, not cannibalization.
No exemptions, waivers, or carve-outs from the enforcement duties are created.

notes: Validation batch (Mayer & Price 2002 appendix positive, pre-registration v1.1). Clean interagency coordination design — scores at or near the floor on this structural instrument, illustrating the scheme's stated divergence from historical/press significance: Mayer & Price code this order significant (wartime press attention, per their criteria), while its governance architecture is largely unremarkable. First-pass coding; not yet cross-coded for inter-coder reliability.
